items.headline: Russia launches over 600 drones and 47 missiles at Ukraine in one of largest strikes since 2022 invasion
items.body: Russia struck 30 locations across Ukraine killing at least 10 people in a massive coordinated attack. The scale signals an escalation that directly affects energy infrastructure and civilian safety for millions across multiple Ukrainian regions.

items.headline: OpenAI shuts down Sora video generation service after user base collapsed by half
items.body: The Sora web and app closed on April 26 after users fell from one million to under 500,000. The shutdown reflects compute cost pressures reshaping which AI services remain economically viable at commercial scale.

items.headline: SK Hynix breaks ground on $13 billion AI memory fabrication plant in South Korea
items.body: Construction began on a factory dedicated to advanced AI chip packaging targeting 2028 completion. The investment cements South Korea's position in supplying memory chips critical to data center expansion worldwide.

items.headline: Middle East conflict cuts regional economic growth forecast by more than half to 1.8 percent
items.body: The World Bank slashed its 2026 MENA growth projection by 2.4 percentage points citing energy route disruptions. Separate analysis warns 8.8 million people across Asia-Pacific risk falling into poverty from resulting energy price shocks.
